summary: Renamed the matching effect translation unit from `ef_061D` to `efspecial` and the global function at 0x80061D70 from `ef_80061D70` to `efSpecial_SpawnSpecial`. The PR kept build and matching metadata in sync by updating `splits.txt`, `symbols.txt`, `configure.py`, the renamed source/header include, and the `efSync_Spawn` extern/call site. It also contains small mechanical formatting/include-order cleanups in Kirby and item files. The decomp.dev bot report showed the expected rename accounting: `main/melee/ef/efspecial` became 100% matched while the old `main/melee/ef/ef_061D` unit became 0%.

key_files.role: Renamed from `src/melee/ef/ef_061D.c`; include changed to `efspecial.h`; function definition renamed to `efSpecial_SpawnSpecial`.
Renamed from `ef_061D.h`; declaration updated from `ef_80061D70` to `efSpecial_SpawnSpecial`.
Updated extern declaration and the `gfx_id < 0x4BA` dispatch path in `efSync_Spawn` to call `efSpecial_SpawnSpecial`.
Renamed the split entry from `melee/ef/ef_061D.c` to `melee/ef/efspecial.c` while preserving the same text/data/sdata/sdata2 address ranges.
Renamed the symbol at `.text:0x80061D70` from `ef_80061D70` to `efSpecial_SpawnSpecial` with the same size `0x1BC0`.
Changed the matching object path from `melee/ef/ef_061D.c` to `melee/ef/efspecial.c`.
Style-only line wrapping for `itOldkuriAttributes* attr` and velocity assignment.
Moved `<placeholder.h>` include near the top of the include block.

what_changed: The function `ef_80061D70(s32 gfx_id, HSD_GObj* gobj, va_list vlist)` was renamed to `efSpecial_SpawnSpecial(...)` without changing the visible signature in the diff.
`config/GALE01/symbols.txt` now maps `.text:0x80061D70` size `0x1BC0` to `efSpecial_SpawnSpecial` instead of `ef_80061D70`.
`config/GALE01/splits.txt` and `configure.py` now reference the renamed `melee/ef/efspecial.c` matching unit.
`efSync_Spawn` now declares and calls `efSpecial_SpawnSpecial` for the `gfx_id < 0x4BA` branch.
The nearby commented-out alternative call in `efsync.c` was also renamed for consistency.
Unrelated mechanical formatting/include-order edits were made in `ftKb_SpecialN.c`, `ithouou.c`, `itoldkuri.c`, and `itpikachuthunder.c`.
smart_moves: The rename was propagated through all important decomp surfaces: source filename, header filename, include, declaration, caller extern, caller invocation, symbols file, splits file, and build object list.
The matching address ranges and symbol size were preserved, making this a naming/config migration rather than a behavioral rewrite.
Updating `configure.py` alongside `splits.txt` avoided leaving the matching build pointed at the old `ef_061D.c` path.
The decomp.dev report verified the expected rename pattern: new `efspecial` unit entries became 100% matched and old `ef_061D` entries became broken because the old unit name disappeared.
decomp_lessons: When renaming a matching translation unit, update `config/GALE01/splits.txt` and the `Object(Matching, ...)` entry in `configure.py` in the same PR.
When replacing an address-based symbol name, update `symbols.txt`, the header declaration, any local externs, call sites, and nearby comments to prevent mixed old/new names.
A decomp.dev report showing new matches for the renamed unit and broken matches for the old unit can be expected rename churn rather than a matching regression.
Prefer a domain-oriented effect name such as `efspecial` / `efSpecial_SpawnSpecial` once the role is known instead of keeping an address-derived placeholder like `ef_061D` / `ef_80061D70`.
The PR body and human review text provide no explanation beyond the title, so the reason for the `Special` naming is not evidenced in this slice.
naming_conventions: Effect source/header filenames use lowercase subsystem/domain form: `efspecial.c` and `efspecial.h`.
Effect function names use a module-style prefix plus verb phrase: `efSpecial_SpawnSpecial`.
Address-derived placeholder names such as `ef_80061D70` and file stems such as `ef_061D` are candidates for replacement when a more meaningful domain name is established.
The header basename should track the source basename; the renamed source includes `"efspecial.h"`.
The existing neighboring prefixes visible in the diff remain distinct: `efLib_`, `efSync_`, `efSpecial_`, and `efAsync_`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Preserve the split address ranges while renaming a matching unit: `.text` remained `0x80061D70` to `0x80063930`, with `.data`, `.sdata`, and `.sdata2` ranges unchanged.
Preserve the symbol address and size while renaming: `efSpecial_SpawnSpecial = .text:0x80061D70` with size `0x1BC0`.
Keep the `va_list` forwarding shape unchanged in `efSync_Spawn`; the diff only renames the callee used for the `gfx_id < 0x4BA` branch.
Use bot match reports carefully after renames: this slice reports `+7104` text bytes and data/sdata/sdata2 matches under `main/melee/ef/efspecial`, mirrored by losses under `main/melee/ef/ef_061D`.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
The PR body was empty, so there is no author-provided rationale beyond the title.
The only comment was the decomp.dev GALE01 report, which showed 4 new matches for `main/melee/ef/efspecial` and 4 broken matches for the old `main/melee/ef/ef_061D` unit.
follow_up_queries: config splits symbols configure.py rename matching object
